purpose: Add Shah (1978) apparent friction factor form to TAMU loop closure library and quantify differences vs existing forms at TAMU conditions

citation_F3_shah_apparent.author: Shah, R.K.
citation_F3_shah_apparent.title: A correlation for laminar hydrodynamic entry length solutions for circular and noncircular ducts

formula_constants.source: Shah (1978) Eq. 15, circular tube, uniform inlet velocity

key_finding: At TAMU loop conditions (x+ = 0.04-0.6), F3_shah_apparent gives 28-99% more total pressure drop than F3_hagenbach. Difference is largest for short segments at high Re (x+ < 0.06) and smallest for long segments at low Re (x+ > 0.5). Hagenbach underestimates total ΔP in the developing region because it captures only the asymptotic momentum defect (K_inf=1.33), not the enhanced distributed friction during profile development.
recommendation: F3_shah_apparent is more physically complete for TAMU loop segments with x+ < 0.2. For x+ > 0.5, F1 (64/Re) is sufficient within ~5%. Buoyancy correction (F4, AGENT-187) is needed for heated/cooled legs.
open_questions_for_user: Should F3_shah_apparent be set as the solver default instead of F1?
Re-compute x+ for test section with D_h=0.021 m (quartz tube bore)?
Implement Muzychka & Yovanovich (2009) for cross-validation once paper is available?
limitations: No GCI bounds on segment lengths (T6 blocked)
Isothermal assumption only; F4 buoyancy correction needed for heated/cooled legs
Shah correlation most accurate for x+ < 0.2; slight overestimate for x+ > 0.3
Assumes uniform (flat) inlet velocity profile; bends may distort this
Muzychka & Yovanovich (2009) not implemented — constants not verified from primary source
